Published: May 3, 2026 | Last reviewed: May 3, 2026

Acne Treatment — Pimples, Skin Treatment Pakistan

TL;DR — Key Takeaways
  • Acne is caused by hormones, excess oil production and bacteria
  • Benzoyl peroxide and salicylic acid are effective OTC options
  • Dairy and high-glycemic foods can worsen acne
  • Never pop pimples — it leaves scars
  • Severe or scarring acne needs a dermatologist and possibly Roaccutane

What Is Acne and Why Does It Happen?

Acne occurs when your skin pores become blocked — oil (sebum), dead skin cells and bacteria (P. acnes) combine to cause inflammation. In Pakistan, heat, sweat, smog and high-sugar diets all make acne worse.

Acne — Common Facts in Pakistan
Most affected age13–25 years — but adult acne also occurs in the 30s and 40s
Common areasFace, back, chest, shoulders
Main triggersHormones, diet, stress, poor cosmetics, heat
Time to see resultsMinimum 6–8 weeks with consistent treatment
Most common type in PakistanInflammatory and hormonal acne

Types of Acne

  • Whiteheads: Small white bumps from closed, clogged pores
  • Blackheads: Open pores where oil oxidises and turns dark
  • Papules: Small red, tender bumps
  • Pustules: Pimples filled with pus
  • Cystic acne: Large, deep, painful — high risk of scarring
  • Hormonal acne: Along the jawline and chin, linked to periods

Causes of Acne

  • Hormones — puberty, periods, PCOS, pregnancy
  • Genetics — family history
  • Diet — dairy, sugar, white bread, fast food
  • Stress — cortisol increases oil production
  • Skincare products — comedogenic creams and heavy makeup
  • Pollution and smog — a major issue in Lahore and Karachi
  • Sweat and heat
  • Mobile phones and pillowcases — bacteria transfer
  • Steroids — found illegally in many "skin whitening" creams

Home Remedies for Acne (Mild Cases)

1. Tea Tree Oil

5% tea tree oil is as effective as benzoyl peroxide for mild acne. Do not apply directly — dilute with a carrier oil (jojoba) and use as a spot treatment.

2. Honey and Cinnamon Mask

1 teaspoon honey + ½ teaspoon cinnamon powder. Apply for 15 minutes. Use twice a week.

3. Aloe Vera Gel

Fresh gel from the plant or pure aloe vera. Soothing and mildly antibacterial.

4. Green Tea

Apply a cooled green tea bag to the face for 5–10 minutes. Helps reduce inflammation.

5. Ice Cubes

Hold ice on a painful pimple for 1–2 minutes to reduce swelling.

Best OTC Treatments — Available in Pakistan

Cleansers:

  • Cetaphil Gentle Skin Cleanser (for sensitive skin)
  • Neutrogena Oil-Free Acne Wash (contains salicylic acid)
  • CeraVe Foaming Cleanser

Active Ingredients:

  • Salicylic acid 0.5–2%: Unclogs pores. Found in Cetaphil DermaControl, Neutrogena
  • Benzoyl peroxide 2.5–5%: Kills acne bacteria. Brands: Persol, Acnomel
  • Niacinamide 5–10%: Reduces inflammation and pigmentation. The Ordinary, Cosrx
  • Adapalene 0.1%: Differin gel — strong retinoid, previously prescription-only, now OTC

Moisturiser and Sunscreen Are Essential:

Many acne treatments dry out the skin — use a non-comedogenic moisturiser (Cetaphil, CeraVe) and SPF 30+ daily. In Pakistan's intense sun, sunscreen prevents scars from darkening further.

Has your acne been persisting for 6 months or more? Consult a dermatologist on Ilaaj AI — get guidance on Roaccutane and prescription treatments from home.

Chat with a Skin Specialist

Diet and Acne

Research shows these foods can worsen acne:

  • Dairy — especially skim milk
  • Sugar — soft drinks, sweets, bakery items
  • White bread, white rice, paratha — high glycaemic index
  • Fast food and fried items
  • Whey protein supplements

Foods that help with acne:

  • Vegetables and fruit (vitamins A, C and zinc)
  • Omega-3 sources (fish, walnuts)
  • Plenty of water
  • Green tea
  • Probiotic yoghurt

Skincare Routine — Realistic for Pakistan

Morning:

  1. Gentle cleanser (Cetaphil)
  2. Toner (optional, alcohol-free)
  3. Oil-free moisturiser
  4. Sunscreen SPF 30+ (non-negotiable)

Night:

  1. Cleanser (remove makeup first)
  2. Treatment (salicylic acid or benzoyl peroxide spot treatment)
  3. Moisturiser
  4. Exfoliant (BHA) 1–2 times per week

What to Avoid

  • Do not pop pimples — scars remain and infection spreads
  • Do not wash your face 5–6 times a day — it dries out the skin
  • Do not scrub daily — causes irritation
  • "Skin whitening" creams (especially Faiza, Stillman's) — contain steroids that damage skin long-term
  • Do not hold your mobile phone directly against your face
  • Change your pillowcase weekly

Hormonal Acne (In Women)

Pimples along the jawline and chin that worsen around periods are hormonal. Treatment options:

  • Birth control pills (Yasmin, Diane-35) — with gynaecologist guidance
  • Spironolactone — effective for hormonal acne, requires a prescription
  • PCOS treatment if diagnosed

Severe Acne — Doctor-Prescribed Treatments

  • Topical retinoids (Tretinoin, Adapalene)
  • Oral antibiotics (Doxycycline, Minocycline) — short-term use only
  • Isotretinoin (Roaccutane) — a game changer for cystic and scarring acne, but requires strict monitoring
  • Chemical peels and laser therapy — available at dermatology clinics

Treating Acne Scars

  • Vitamin C serum and niacinamide reduce pigmentation
  • SPF 50+ sunscreen prevents scars from darkening
  • Microneedling, chemical peels or laser — at a dermatologist clinic
  • Patience is key — minimum 3–6 months for visible improvement

When to See a Doctor

  • No improvement after 8–12 weeks of OTC treatment
  • Cystic or nodular acne (deep, painful)
  • Scarring is forming
  • Hormonal pattern (jawline, chin, worsens with periods)
  • Acne is affecting your mental health
  • Acne with PCOS symptoms (irregular periods, excess hair growth)

Conclusion

Acne is treatable, but patience and consistency are essential. Any treatment takes a minimum of 6–8 weeks to show results. Stay away from steroid-based "whitening creams" — they destroy your skin over time. On Ilaaj AI, you can consult a dermatologist and get a personalised prescription, product list and lifestyle plan — all from home.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best treatment for pimples?

For mild acne: salicylic acid 1–2% face wash + niacinamide serum + non-comedogenic moisturiser + SPF 30+ sunscreen daily. Spot-treat with benzoyl peroxide 2.5%. Results take 6–8 weeks. Severe acne requires a dermatologist for topical retinoids, antibiotics, or Roaccutane. Online dermatologist consultations are available on Ilaaj AI.

How do you get rid of pimples on your face?

Step one: stop using comedogenic products (heavy oils, thick makeup). Cleanse gently twice daily. Apply an active ingredient (salicylic acid or benzoyl peroxide). Never skip moisturiser and sunscreen. Do not pop pimples. Stay consistent for 6–8 weeks. Reduce dairy and sugar, drink more water. If there is no improvement, see a dermatologist.

How do acne scars fade?

Mild dark spots (post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation) fade on their own within 3–6 months — daily SPF 50+ sunscreen is essential. Vitamin C serum and niacinamide speed up fading. Deep scars (icepick, boxcar) require microneedling, chemical peels or laser treatment at a dermatology clinic. Roaccutane helps prevent scarring in severe acne cases.

What is the treatment for hormonal acne?

Hormonal acne in women appears along the jawline and chin around the time of periods. Treatment options include birth control pills (Yasmin, Diane-35) or spironolactone (anti-androgen), prescribed by a gynaecologist. PCOS testing is important as it often co-exists. Topical treatments alone are usually insufficient — systemic medication is needed. Female doctors are available on Ilaaj AI.

Are skin whitening creams safe?

Most whitening creams available in Pakistan (Faiza, Stillman's, Goree, etc.) contain illegal steroids. They may lighten skin initially, but after 6 months cause skin thinning, breakouts, redness, broken capillaries and permanent damage. Avoid them completely. For a healthy glow, use sunscreen, vitamin C serum and niacinamide.
